input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11, targeting Android + Windows.

---

## Phase 1: Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install latest .NET 8 SDK via winget (MAUI's primary supported SDK)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Refresh your PATH
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Phase 2: Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads (see what's offered)
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls in android, maccatalyst, ios dependencies automatically)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Phase 3: Android SDK & JDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components under the .NET Android bindings, but you need a full Android SDK for emulators and build tools.

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK 17 (required by Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Set JAVA_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
    "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17*" -replace '\*',
    (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\" -Filter "jdk-17*" -Directory |
     Select-Object -First 1).Name.Split('\')[-1],
    "User")

# Simpler — discover and set dynamically:
$jdkPath = (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\" -Filter "jdk-17*" -Directory |
            Select-Object -First 1).FullName
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $jdkPath, "User")
$env:JAVA_HOME = $jdkPath

# Install Android Studio CLI tools (or standalone command-line tools)
winget install Google.AndroidStudio --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# --- OR install standalone command-line tools only (lighter) ---
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only
# Extract to C:\Android\cmdline-tools\latest\

# Set ANDROID_HOME
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidHome

# Add platform-tools and cmdline-tools to PATH
$newPath = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin;$androidHome\platform-tools;$androidHome\emulator"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",
    [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User") + ";$newPath", "User")
$env:Path += ";$newPath"
```

### Install required Android SDK components

```powershell
# List all available packages (discover versions dynamically)
sdkmanager --list

# Find the latest build-tools, platform, etc.
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools" | Select-Object -Last 5
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -Last 5

# Install required components (.NET 8 MAUI targets API 34 by default)
# Check what your MAUI version actually needs:
dotnet new maui -n TempCheck --output TempCheck
Select-String -Path "TempCheck\TempCheck.csproj" -Pattern "TargetFramework"
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force TempCheck

# Install SDK packages (adjust API level based on above output)
sdkmanager "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"
sdkmanager "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"
sdkmanager "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
sdkmanager --licenses
# (Type 'y' for each prompt)
```

### Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# Create an AVD
avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_7"

# List AVDs to confirm
avdmanager list avd

# Launch emulator (test it)
emulator -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Phase 4: Windows Target (Windows App SDK)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading/debugging)
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# → Toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the settings window that opens

# The Windows App SDK is pulled in by NuGet at build time,
# but you need the runtime for local testing:
winget search "Windows App Runtime"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppRuntime.1.5 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Enable long paths (avoids build errors with deep node_modules/android paths)
Set-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" `
    -Name "LongPathsEnabled" -Value 1 -Type DWord
```

## Phase 5: Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Create new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## Phase 6: Verify Everything

```powershell
# Full diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
java -version
sdkmanager --list_installed
avdmanager list avd

# Optional: install MAUI check tool for comprehensive validation
dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check
maui-check
```

---

**Key version-discovery tips:**
- `dotnet workload search` — find workload names dynamically
- `sdkmanager --list` — discover all available Android SDK versions
- `Select-String` on the generated `.csproj` — see which API level MAUI targets
- `winget search` — find exact package IDs for any dependency
- `maui-check` — auto-detects and reports missing/mismatched components
data.reasoningText:
The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.

Let me think about what's needed for .NET MAUI development:

1. .NET SDK (latest .NET 8 or .NET 9)
2. MAUI workload
3. Android SDK, NDK, emulator
4. Java JDK (for Android)
5. Windows App SDK (for Windows target)
6. Visual Studio or VS Code (optional but common)


7. Environment variables and path configuration

Now I'll organize all the PowerShell commands they'll need to execute in the right order, starting with the .NET SDK installation and then moving through each dependency.
